title: Rseikharhl

Jason Fry [[Special:Diff/3187155|confirmed]] that [[Rseik]] was the Rseikharhl homeworld in an edit on [[Wookieepedia]], with authorship of the edit later confirmed at {{JCFCite|url=posts/31129018|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Guide to Warfare by Jason Fry and a pseudonymous Scotsman|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2010-07-29|time=11:59 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200802120642/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-by-jason-fry-and-a-pseudonymous-scotsman.30937840/page-37}}</ref>
|habitat=

}}
The '''Rseikharhl''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from [[Rseik]], which was located in the [[Rseik sector/Legends|Rseik sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. By [[214 BBY]], the Rseikharhl were technologically advanced and were able to provide [[Antidote/Legends|antidotes]] for the [[Bordal Contagion]] that [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] shipments were destroyed by [[Fujari]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. Following the Republic's inability to aid the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], the Rseikharhl, [[Kett Shipping]], the [[Ma'ar Shaddam Weaponsmiths Guild]], and other interests from the area set up a meeting with a [[Unidentified Balmorran Trade Federation representative|representative]] from the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], which ended with many of the organizations in the area becoming Trade Federation members. The Rseikharhl developed [[Droid/Legends|droids]] such as the [[LE-VO law enforcement droid]], which they used in law enforcement across the entire [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the species was under Imperial jurisdiction, and their droids had spread throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], especially to [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] on the galactic frontier.

==Biology and appearance==
The Rseikharhl were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Cynabar" />

==Society and culture==
[[File:LE-VO_law_enforcement_droid.jpg|thumb|right|upright|A Rseikharhl-desgined LE-VO law enforcement droid]]
Due in large part to the [[LE-VO law enforcement droid]]s that the [[Rseikharhl Droid Group/Legends|Rseikharhl Droid Group]] produced,<ref name="EOTE">[[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Core Rulebook]]</ref> the Rseikharhl effectively managed law enforcement across their home [[Sector/Legends|sector]],<ref name="Cynabar" /> the [[Rseik sector/Legends|Rseik sector]],<ref name="Showdown">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-6-showdown-at-naboo|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 6: Showdown at Naboo}}</ref> which was located next to the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]],<ref name="Cynabar" /> [[Tarabba sector/Legends|Tarabba]], [[Tamarin sector/Legends|Tamarin]] and [[Svivreni sector/Legends|Svivreni sectors]].<ref name="Outer Rim Extra">{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/atlas/news20090910/index.html|text=''Essential Atlas'' Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> During the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Bordal Contagion]], the Rseikharhl used their own resources to construct [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratories]] and [[Antidote/Legends|antidotes]] to help stop the plague, which was [[Death/Legends|killing]] the [[Bordal]]i.<ref name="Showdown" /> While under the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] jurisdiction,<ref name="EOTE" /> the technologically adept species<ref name="Cynabar" /> took care designing and [[Programmer/Legends|programming]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that did not deviate from Imperial codes and only used force when necessary.<ref name="EOTE" /> The Rseikharhl believed preventing wrongful deaths was more important than stopping a few criminals from taking advantage of the droids' insistence on the least violent solution. Due to the Rseikharhl's beliefs<ref name="Galladinium">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]''</ref> and Imperial laws, the species' droids were normally equipped with non-lethal [[Stun/Legends|stun]] weaponry.<ref name="EOTE" />

==History==
===Pirates and the Trade Federation===
The Rseikharhl species was native to [[Rseik]] in the [[Rseik system/Legends|Rseik system]],<ref name="JasonFry" /> which was located within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> By [[214 BBY]], the sector of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] containing the Rseikharhl [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was known as the Rseik sector. The sector was sparsely populated and plagued by many different groups of pirates. The Galactic Republic, who ruled a large portion of the galaxy at that [[Time/Legends|time]], had not paid much attention to the area for around a [[Standard century/Legends|century]]. In 214 BBY, a deadly plague known as the [[Bordal Contagion]] began killing the Bordali. The Republic developed an antidote for the disease, but the [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] sent to deliver the shipment was attacked by [[Fujari]] pirates. When the Republic did not send additional aid, the Rseikharhl constructed laboratories and synthesized the antidote for the Bordali, but they were not able to produce the antidote before millions of Bordali died to the plague.<ref name="Showdown" />

Following the Bordal Contagion, the Rseikharhl, the [[Ma'ar Shaddam Weaponsmiths Guild]], [[Kett Shipping]], and other groups from the area discussed the situation and decided to arrange a meeting with a representative from the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], a powerful galactic corporation. After a single [[Standard day/Legends|day]] of meeting with the [[Unidentified Balmorran Trade Federation representative|representative]] on [[Syned]] and discussing the [[Planet/Legends|planets]], their financial situations, and the various pirate groups in the area, the organizations that attended the meeting became members of the Trade Federation. One Rseikharhl company that joined the Trade Federation was [[Rseikharhl Hullspace]]. Shortly afterward, [[Warship/Legends|warships]] from the [[Trade Defense Force]] overwhelmed the pirates in the area. Eventually the Trade Federation had control over the entire Rseik sector.<ref name="Showdown" />

===Under the Empire===
By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] fought between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], the Rseikharhl were considered a technologically advanced species and fell under Imperial rule. During that period, the species had expanded its reach to police the entire Rseik sector, often with the aid of droids of Rseikharhl design. One such droid model was the bipedal LE-VO law enforcement droid,<ref name="Cynabar" /> which was designed by the Rseikharhl Droid Group.<ref name="EOTE" /> The model's non-lethal programming was remarkably successful, as the droids secured over 22,000 arrests without causing any [[Death/Legends|fatalities]] across a ten-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] period. Due to that record, the Empire permitted the Rseikharhl to continue using the droids to police their sector despite the model's advanced nature.<ref name="Cynabar" />

The Rseikharhl design could be found throughout the galaxy, operating as part of [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police forces]] and private security outfits.<ref name="EOTE" /> The droids were particularly common on many frontier worlds in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="Cynabar" /> such as [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]],<ref name="SWM10">''[[Star Wars Missions 10: Showdown in Mos Eisley]]''</ref> acting as local constabularies. At some point during the war between the Rebel Alliance and the Empire, the droids were available for sale at 9,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] from both the illegal [[Cynabar]]'s [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog|Droid Datalog]]<ref name="Cynabar" /> and the pro-Imperial [[Unidentified Galladinium president|Galladinium's]] [[Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology|Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology]].<ref name="Galladinium" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Rseikharhl were created for the [[1995]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', in an entry on the LE-VO law enforcement droid created by [[Michael Kogge|Mike Kogge]] for [[The Fantastic Technology Contest]].<ref name="Galladinium" /> In [[1997]], the droid and the Rseikharhl were mentioned again in ''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'', which was written by [[Drew Campbell]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]].<ref name="Cynabar" /> The Rseikharhl homeworld was confirmed to be Rseik by ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' co-author [[Jason Fry]] in [[2010]].<ref name="JasonFry" /> The Rseikharhl were originally going to be mentioned in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', a [[2012]] reference book written by Jason Fry and [[Paul R. Urquhart]], but the section that included them was cut. However, in [[2014]], the cut section was released in "Showdown at Naboo," the sixth part of [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut]].<ref name="Showdown" />

"Showdown on Naboo" mentioned that the Galactic Republic had not thought about the Rseik sector in around a century, but the article did not confirm whether the sector and the Rseikharhl were under Republic jurisdiction. The article also mentioned Rseik, but did not specify whether it was a planet or another type of stellar body.<ref name="Showdown" /> The [[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Core Rulebook]] identified the species' main sector as the "Rseikharhl" sector,<ref name="EOTE" /> instead of the Rseik sector referred to in the West End Games books<ref name="Galladinium" /><ref name="Cynabar" /> and "Showdown on Naboo."<ref name="Showdown" /> This article assumes that Rseik sector spelling is correct. In ''Galladinium's Fantastic Technology''<ref name="Galladinium" /> and ''Cynabar's Fantastic Technology'', "Rseikharhls" is given as the plural form of the species name,<ref name="Cynabar" /> while "Showdown at Naboo" gave "Rseikharhl" as the plural form. As "Showdown at Naboo" is the most recent source,<ref name="Showdown" /> this article treats "Rseikharhl" as the correct plural spelling for the species name.
